query: /bruhs:spawn code-runner — an API that executes AI-generated Python submitted by users
context: Fresh project (not a monorepo). The whole job of the API is running AI-generated / untrusted user code, so a sandbox is squarely in scope. Linear is configured.
expected_behavior: During Step 2e stack selection, spawn presents the Sandboxing question (header 'Sandboxing') offering Daytona, because the project is an API that runs AI-generated / untrusted code
If Daytona is selected, the written bruhs:state stack includes "sandboxing": ["Daytona"] (the selected value)
derive_stack_rules.py emits the Daytona rules block into bruhs:rules — run AI/untrusted code inside a sandbox, reuse-vs-per-call, delete + idle auto-stop, secrets via env, bounded concurrency
Both the bruhs:state and bruhs:rules blocks are written to CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md via the sync helpers
failure_modes: Never offers a Sandboxing / Daytona option despite the project being about running untrusted code
Selects Daytona but omits the "sandboxing" key from the stack JSON, so derive_stack_rules.py can't pick it up
Daytona selected but no Daytona rules appear in the bruhs:rules block (signal not iterated in collect_signals)
Invents a sandboxing provider that isn't an offered option
